title: Torches
text: Torches are essential items for navigating dark areas of the dungeons. Without a torch or light source, the screen becomes nearly black and enemies are harder to see. Torches burn out after a limited time. A Tinderbox or Lighter (Yesquero) is required to ignite a torch. Running out of torches in a dark area can be fatal.

title: Tinderbox and Lighter (Yesquero)
text: A Tinderbox or Lighter (Yesquero in Spanish) is required to ignite torches and certain environmental objects. Without one, torches cannot be lit. These items are found in early dungeon areas and are one of the first priorities for any run.

title: Arm Guards
text: Arm Guards are accessories that protect the wearer's arms from being severed by enemy attacks. They are especially useful against Guards, whose cleaver attacks can remove limbs. Arm Guards do not prevent all arm damage but significantly reduce the chance of dismemberment.

title: Blue Vial
text: Blue vials are rare consumables that permanently increase a character's attack stat. They are one of the few ways to permanently improve character stats in the game, alongside equipment upgrades and specific entity interactions. Blue vials are highly valuable and should be used strategically on the character who will benefit most.

title: Healing Items
text: Common healing items in Fear & Hunger include Blue Grass (Blue herb), which heals HP, Light Blue Vials that restore moderate HP, and Red Vials that heal HP and cure infection. Green herbs cure infection and poison. Cloth fragments treat bleeding. Mixing herbs at specific locations can create more potent healing items.

title: Crow of Ma'habre
text: The Crow of Ma'habre is a mysterious merchant NPC found in specific dungeon locations. It sells rare and valuable items including books, vials, and equipment. The Crow appears in fixed locations and may offer different wares depending on the player's progress.

title: Party Recruitment
text: The player can recruit NPCs to join their party throughout the dungeons. Recruitable characters include the Girl (found in a cage), Cahara (the mercenary), D'arce (the knight), Ragnvaldr (the outlander), and Enki (the dark priest). Each recruitable character has unique skills and dialogue. Recruitment often requires specific conditions or dialogue choices.

title: Ritual Circles
text: Ritual circles serve multiple purposes: they save the game, and in some cases allow the player to heal the party or perform specific rituals. Resting at certain ritual circles can restore HP and Mind. Ritual circles are marked by a distinctive symbol on the ground and are often found in safe rooms or hidden alcoves.
